players.name: Brice Samba
players.bio: Born in Congo and raised in France, Samba is a commanding shot-stopper who reinvented himself at Lens before joining Rennes. With just four caps, this World Cup is his chance to prove he belongs among Europe's elite.
players.facts: Samba saved three penalties in one shootout for Lens vs PSG in the 2023 Coupe de France semis. Ice. Cold.

players.name: Malo Gusto
players.bio: Explosive and energetic, Gusto is Chelsea's dynamo at right-back with an eye for overlapping runs. Still only 23, this Lyon academy graduate is living proof that full-backs can be box-office entertainment.
players.facts: Gusto was born the day after France beat Brazil in the 1997 Tournoi de France. Destiny was always calling.

players.name: Lucas Digne
players.bio: A seasoned left-back with 58 caps and Premier League pedigree, Digne's delivery from set-pieces is genuinely dangerous. The veteran of multiple tournaments, he brings calm authority to France's backline.
players.facts: Digne has played for PSG, Roma, Barcelona, Everton and Aston Villa — basically a Grand Tour of European football.

players.name: Dayot Upamecano
players.bio: Powerful, pacy and occasionally heart-stopping, Upamecano has become a key figure at Bayern Munich and for Les Bleus. A runner-up at Qatar 2022, he arrives at 2026 determined to go one better.
players.facts: Upamecano grew up in Évreux, the same Normandy city that produced former France international Steve Mandanda's roots.

players.name: Jules Koundé
players.bio: Technically brilliant and positionally versatile, Kounde can play centre-back or right-back with equal composure. A World Cup finalist in 2022, the Barcelona defender is one of football's most complete defenders.
players.facts: Kounde is a serious art enthusiast and has spoken openly about painting — truly a Renaissance man at the back.

players.name: Manu Koné
players.bio: A dynamic box-to-box midfielder with an engine that never quits, Koné has thrived under José Mourinho and successors at Roma. Still only 25, this is his first World Cup and he intends to make some noise.
players.facts: Koné represented France at youth level all the way from U16 to U21 before earning his senior debut.

players.name: Ousmane Dembélé
players.bio: When Dembélé is on form, he's unplayable — blistering pace, trickery, and an end product that now actually arrives. After injury-plagued years at Barcelona, he's flourished at PSG and looks ready to dazzle in North America.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Vapor XIII Elite FG
players.facts: Dembélé is ambidextrous — he can shoot with both feet at terrifying speed. Defenders have been warned repeatedly.

players.name: Aurélien Tchouaméni
players.bio: Composed, intelligent, and built like a tank, Tchouaméni has become Real Madrid and France's midfield shield. A World Cup finalist at just 22 in Qatar, he's now the senior statesman of a very talented engine room.
players.facts: Tchouaméni missed his penalty in the 2022 World Cup final shootout but still won the Champions League with Madrid in 2024.

players.name: Marcus Thuram
players.bio: Son of French legend Lilian, Marcus has carved his own legacy at Inter Milan with powerful, selfless centre-forward play. Born in Parma, he carries Italian flair and French grit — a genuinely unique combination.
players.facts: Marcus Thuram was born in Parma while his father Lilian was playing for the Italian club. Full circle football.

players.name: Kylian Mbappé
players.bio: Quite simply one of the greatest footballers of his generation. France's all-time top scorer, World Cup winner in 2018, finalist in 2022 — Mbappé arrives at 2026 hungry for the trophy that defines legacies.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Superfly VII Elite FG
players.facts: Mbappé became only the second player after Pelé to score in a World Cup final as a teenager, in 2018.

players.name: Michael Olise
players.bio: Born in London to a French mother and Nigerian father, Olise chose Les Bleus and hasn't looked back. An elegant, creative winger with an eye for goal, he's Bayern's star turn and France's most exciting new weapon.
players.facts: Olise chose France over England and Nigeria. England fans are still not over it — and probably never will be.

players.name: Bradley Barcola
players.bio: Quick, direct and full of confidence, Barcola has become PSG's left-wing revelation since arriving from Lyon. Still only 23, his pace and dribbling make him a genuine headache for any defence in the world.
players.facts: Barcola grew up supporting Lyon and came through their academy — PSG then signed him and the rest is history.

players.name: N'Golo Kanté
players.bio: The most beloved midfielder of his generation, Kanté's tenacity, humility and energy are the stuff of legend. Now at Fenerbahçe, the 2018 World Cup winner makes a fairytale return to the tournament stage at 35.
players.facts: Kanté reportedly does his own laundry at training grounds and once got lost driving to a stadium. A true icon.

players.name: Adrien Rabiot
players.bio: Tall, technically gifted and impossible to dispossess, Rabiot has reinvented himself at AC Milan after leaving Juventus. A 2022 World Cup finalist with seven international goals, he's a big-game midfielder with real pedigree.
players.facts: Rabiot famously refused to be a standby player for France's 2018 World Cup squad. He still won it anyway — sort of.

players.name: Ibrahima Konaté
players.bio: Imperious in the air and ferocious in the tackle, Konaté has become a cornerstone of Liverpool's defence. Tall, athletic and deceptively calm on the ball, this is arguably his best chance yet at a World Cup medal.
players.facts: Konaté won the Champions League with Liverpool in 2024-25, cementing his status as one of Europe's elite defenders.

players.name: Mike Maignan
players.bio: Born in French Guiana, Magic Mike is widely regarded as one of the world's best goalkeepers — electric reflexes, commanding presence, and a leader by nature. France's No.1 and the last line of a very formidable defence.
players.facts: Maignan kept 16 clean sheets in Serie A in 2023-24. Attackers reportedly cross themselves before facing him.

players.name: William Saliba
players.bio: Cool, composed and seemingly unrattleable, Saliba has been Arsenal's defensive cornerstone for years. The Bondy-born centre-back — from the same Paris suburb as Mbappé — brings elite reading of the game to France's squad.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Vapor XIII Elite FG
players.facts: Saliba and Mbappé both grew up in Bondy, Seine-Saint-Denis. That's one remarkably productive suburb of Paris.

players.name: Warren Zaïre-Emery
players.bio: At just 20, Zaïre-Emery is already a PSG first-team regular with Champions League and international experience. A technically superb midfielder who plays with the poise of someone twice his age — frightening potential.
players.facts: Zaïre-Emery was born in 2006 — the year France last reached a World Cup final. He's come to fix that stat.

players.name: Théo Hernandez
players.bio: Théo is everything a modern attacking left-back should be — rapid, aggressive, and loves a thunderbolt. Brother of Lucas, he's now at Al-Hilal in Saudi Arabia but remains a key figure in France's ambitious plans.
players.facts: Théo and Lucas Hernandez are the first brothers to play together in a World Cup final, both featuring in 2022.

players.name: Désiré Doué
players.bio: Silky, inventive and blessed with extraordinary close control, Doucé has lit up PSG and the French youth ranks. At just 21 with two senior goals already, he arrives at his first World Cup as one of Europe's hottest prospects.
players.facts: Doucé won the French Young Player of the Year award at just 18 while still at Rennes. A talent right on schedule.

players.name: Lucas Hernandez
players.bio: A warrior defender whose career has been hampered by serious injuries, Lucas Hernandez showed immense courage returning from an ACL tear at the 2022 World Cup. Now fit and determined, he wants his winner's medal on the pitch.
players.facts: Lucas tore his ACL just minutes into France's opening game of Qatar 2022 — yet still collected a runners-up medal.

players.name: Jean-Philippe Mateta
players.bio: Tall, powerful and clinical, Mateta has been Crystal Palace's unlikely hero in the Premier League, scoring goals when it matters. With two goals in just four caps, this World Cup could be his global coming-out party.
players.facts: Mateta once went on loan to Mainz, Le Havre AND Toulon before finally finding his home at Crystal Palace.

players.name: Robin Risser
players.bio: The youngest in France's squad and uncapped at senior level, Risser is a supremely talented young goalkeeper from Alsace making the step up from Lens. This is his first taste of a World Cup — and he's just soaking it in.
players.facts: Risser was born in Colmar — the birthplace of Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi, the sculptor of the Statue of Liberty.

players.name: Rayan Cherki
players.bio: A generational talent who burst onto the scene at Lyon as a teenager, Cherki is now terrorising opponents at Manchester City. Quick-thinking, creative and deadly in tight spaces, he's the wildcard France's attack craves.
players.facts: Cherki scored his first Ligue 1 goal at just 16 years old — making him one of the youngest ever scorers in the league.

players.name: Maghnes Akliouche
players.bio: Tricky, tenacious and hard to pin down, Akliouche has been one of Monaco's most exciting players in recent seasons. With nine caps and counting, this World Cup represents his biggest stage — and he has the talent to shine.
players.facts: Akliouche is of Algerian descent and was courted by Algeria before committing to France. Les Bleus won that battle.

players.name: Maxence Lacroix
players.bio: A no-nonsense, commanding centre-back who has impressed in the Premier League with Crystal Palace, Lacroix brings physicality and aerial dominance to France's defensive options. Reliable cover with an eye for the main chance.
players.facts: Lacroix developed at Sochaux before moving to Germany's Wolfsburg, where he became one of the Bundesliga's best CBs.
